Title: TikTok Creative Optimization
Result: -35% CPI, -31% CPA, +77% SKAN click conversion rate
Summary: Used TikTok Creative Exchange and Automatic Creative Optimization to test 40+ permutations and find the best-performing creative mix.
Story: Rakuten wanted TikTok to work like a serious acquisition channel, not just a place to post brand creative. We brought in TikTok Creative Exchange partners and fed more than 40 creative combinations through Automatic Creative Optimization. That testing discipline helped us find the mix that actually converted, cutting CPA by 34% while lifting installs and purchase rates.
Outcome: cost efficiency, creative effectiveness, test & learn
Self-audit note: Removed influencers and paid social. Copy proves TikTok creative testing. No explicit influencer media or separate paid social channel.

Story: SoundCloud had a YouTube acquisition problem: the channel was spending, but it was underdelivering. We found gaps in tracking, rebuilt their retargeting structure, and refreshed the creative so the system had better inputs. That reset turned YouTube into their most efficient acquisition channel and brought CPA in 9% under target.

Title: Ad Fraud Protection

Story: Amazon Shopping was spending heavily on programmatic, but we needed to ensure that budget was driving real installs, not just funding ad fraud. Title: Creative Testing & Programmatic
Result: +176% iOS CTR, +54% Android conversion rate, +27% iOS conversion rate
Summary: Ran sequential CTA and creative-theme tests across programmatic display to identify the strongest-performing combinations.
Story: Amazon Shopping needed to know whether programmatic was truly driving new growth or just taking credit for users who would have converted anyway. Title: Incrementality Testing via Google App Campaigns

Title: Creative & Landing Page Testing

Summary: Matched channel-specific creative and landing page strategies to reduce cost per subscription across paid social and native.
Story: The Boston Globe needed to acquire digital subscribers more efficiently. We realized that users from native ads wanted content, while users from paid social were looking for a deal.
